exercises.prompt: The author writes « je pense que… ». In your résumé, you write…

options.text: « je pense que… » — the summary keeps the author's voice
« l'auteur pense que… » — always report from outside
nothing — opinions are cut

exercises.explanation: Unlike the synthèse, the résumé speaks AS the author: je stays je.

exercises.prompt: What gets pruned FIRST when compressing?

options.text: The examples
The thesis
The connectors

exercises.explanation: Examples die first, then redites and digressions. The thesis and the connectors survive.

exercises.prompt: The compression tool you already know.
exercises.template: « Parce que les ventes ont baissé » se condense en « {{b1}} la baisse des ventes ».
exercises.explanation: Nominalisation + du fait de: a clause becomes a noun phrase — résumé gold.
Élaguer = to prune — the résumé's favourite verb.

exercises.prompt: Which is FORBIDDEN in a résumé?

options.text: Adding your own commentary on the text
Keeping the author's conclusion
Using sharper connectors than the original

exercises.explanation: No commentary, no remixing, no copying — compression only.

exercises.prompt: What always survives the cut? (one word)
exercises.template: Les exemples meurent, la {{b1}} survit toujours.
exercises.explanation: La thèse — the central claim — is the one thing a résumé can never lose.

exercises.prompt: Match each résumé term to its English meaning.
Put the words in order: "Summarising is not betraying."
Listen to the teacher's three commandments. Which list is right?
exercises.transcript: Trois règles : même voix, même ordre, un quart de la longueur — et comptez vos mots !

options.text: Same voice, same order, a quarter of the length — count your words
New voice, new order, half the length
Any voice, any order, as short as possible

exercises.explanation: The résumé contract: voix, ordre, quota.

exercises.prompt: An original and its résumé. Read both and answer the questions.
exercises.passageFr:
ORIGINAL (60 mots) : « Je crois, et les statistiques de plusieurs instituts le confirment d'ailleurs année après année, que la lecture sur écran, dont on annonçait qu'elle tuerait le livre — souvenez-vous des prophéties des années 2000 ! —, a en réalité créé de nouveaux lecteurs, plus jeunes, plus nombreux, et étonnamment plus fidèles. »

RÉSUMÉ (20 mots) : « Je crois, statistiques à l'appui, que la lecture sur écran, loin de tuer le livre, a créé de nouveaux lecteurs fidèles. »
exercises.passageEn:
ORIGINAL (60 words): "I believe — and several institutes' statistics confirm it year after year — that screen reading, which was supposed to kill the book (remember the prophecies of the 2000s!), has in fact created new readers: younger, more numerous, surprisingly more loyal."

SUMMARY (20 words): "I believe, statistics in hand, that screen reading, far from killing the book, has created loyal new readers."

options.text: The aside about the 2000s prophecies and the redundant details
The author's thesis
The first-person voice

questions.prompt: Why does the résumé still say « je crois »?

options.text: Because the résumé keeps the author's voice and point of view
Because the summariser agrees with the author
It's a mistake — it should say « l'auteur croit »

exercises.prompt: Your résumé is at 35% of the original length. You should…

options.text: prune further — the quota is about a quarter (±10%)
submit it — longer is safer
add an example back for balance

exercises.explanation: The length instruction is part of the exercise: ~25% ±10%. Count your words.

exercises.prompt: Listen, then choose what it means.
exercises.transcript: Le résumé garde la thèse, l'ordre et la voix de l'auteur.

options.text: The summary keeps the thesis, the order and the author's voice.
Prune the examples first, then the repetitions.
If the author says I, your summary says I.
a summary
to condense
to prune

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing word (from this unit's vocabulary).
exercises.template: Le {{b1}} fait un quart du texte.
exercises.explanation: « Le résumé fait un quart du texte. » — a summary.

exercises.prompt: Match each word to its English meaning.
Put the words in order: “Prune the examples first, then the repetitions.”
Speaking practice — listen, then say the sentence aloud and record yourself:
exercises.targetFr: Le résumé garde la thèse, l'ordre et la voix de l'auteur.
exercises.targetEn: The summary keeps the thesis, the order and the author's voice.

exercises.prompt: Speaking practice — say this sentence aloud and record yourself:
exercises.targetFr: Si l'auteur dit je, votre résumé dit je.
exercises.targetEn: If the author says I, your summary says I.
